channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Elon Musk faces more conflict-of-interest questions after DOGE fires FDA staff reviewing Neuralink
item.description: Since the moment the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) was first proposed, Elon Musk’s critics have warned that the world’s richest man was at risk of making decisions that could be a conflict of interest, given his multiple business operations.
item.dc:creator: Chris Morris, Fast Company

item.title: OpenAI cofounder Ilya Sutskever’s new AI startup is fundraising with a $30 billion valuation
item.description: A new artificial intelligence company from one of the cofounders of OpenAI is quickly becoming one of the most highly valued AI firms in an increasingly crowded marketplace.
item.dc:creator: Chris Morris, Fast Company

item.title: How to maximize hard-dollar ROI on security technology investments
item.description: Fresh research from PWC has found that CIOs are far less likely to say that the IT function is prepared for reinvention, compared to 2023. The drop is considerable: 20 percentage points in just 12 months indicates a significant loss of confidence that IT is ready to modernize.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Fancy a drone destroyer? Someone transformed a Framework 16 laptop into a UAV killer thanks to a custom SDR module
item.description: A hardware designer has developed a drone jammer using a custom SDR module for the Framework modular laptop.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: How drones may be a big help to first responders in the future
item.description: A recent challenge sponsored by NIST tasked drone designers with creating a system to help first responders map indoor environments. The post How drones may be a big help to first responders in the future first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Burchett compares DOGE staffers to young WWII soldiers
item.description: Rep. Tim Burchett (R-Tenn.) on Monday compared Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) staffers to young World War II soldiers. Burchett was pressed by CNN’s Pamela Brown on DOGE’s reported attempt to enter an IRS system, as well as its overall access to data.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Musk's xAI claims new Grok 3 model performs better than rivals
item.description: Elon Musk’s artificial intelligence (AI) company, xAI, claimed Tuesday that its newest model outperforms similar products from competitors like OpenAI and DeepSeek.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Strategies for telecom executives: navigating the OPEX conundrum
item.description: As the market becomes increasingly competitive and technology evolves rapidly, telecommunications industry leaders must find ways to achieve more with less — managing operating expenses (OPEX) without sacrificing critical capabilities.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: 2025 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Want to transform networking? Empower the missing users
item.description: As 2025 kicked off, I wrote a column about the network vendor landscape – specifically, which networking players will step up and put their efforts into finding new applications with solid business benefits that could enable a network transformation.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Apple’s celebrating the 2025 MLS season kicking off with a custom logo for each and every team
item.description: Apple is putting a fresh spin on the Apple TV logo. Well, actually, there are 30 fresh spins, one for each team in Major League Soccer ahead of the 30th season kicking off.
item.dc:creator: jacob.krol@futurenet.com (Jacob Krol), TechRadar

item.title: Kai Cenat plans to launch a ‘streaming university’ to teach aspiring creators
item.description: Kai Cenat is launching a “streaming university.” Cenat announced his plans during a February 13 stream, explaining how he wants to help streamers both big and small learn from his success. “I’m going to rent out a university over a course of a weekend. It will be streaming university.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Trump and EU Commission set course for confrontation on big tech
item.description: width="2193" height="1234" sizes="(max-width: 2193px) 100vw, 2193px">With the second Trump administration, very different cultures are once again clashing in the transatlantic relationship. rawf8 – shutterstock.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Radiografía del CFO: de la adopción de la IA a la transformación del rol
item.description: La figura del director financiero (CFO, por sus siglas en inglés) en las organizaciones medias españolas se ha modernizado y dejado atrás su perfil tradicional, traspasando las fronteras de la mera gestión financiera para alzarse como un actor estratégico, especialmente tras la consolidación de la d
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Sonic the Hedgehog 3 has finally raced its way onto Paramount Plus, and I'm gutted that I won't be able to stream it
item.description: The third Sonic the Hedgehog movie has made its official streaming debut, but only in certain countries.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: Telefónica Trials Quantum-Safe Communications in Madrid Hospitals
item.description: Telefónica, in collaboration with Vithas and LuxQuanta, has successfully implemented a quantum-safe communication system between two hospitals in Madrid using quantum key distribution (QKD) technology.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Serverless was never a cure-all
item.description: An obituary for serverless computing might read something like this: In loving memory of serverless computing, born from the fervent hopes of developers and cloud architects alike.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Huawei just unveiled fully waterproof IP57-rated open earbuds and I think they could be the bargain of 2025
item.description: Like the look of Beats' new Powerbeats Pro 2 but want to bag a fully-waterproof design –and a significant saving? Huawei's got just the thing…
item.dc:creator: becky.scarrott@futurenet.com (Becky Scarrott), TechRadar

item.title: The Future of Indoor Space: Harnessing Spatial Intelligence to Reduce Energy Consumption
item.description: Discover how campus leaders are leveraging AI and body heat sensing technology to understand human behavior in indoor spaces, and how these insights can be applied to reduce energy consumption in workplaces.
item.dc:creator: Honghao Deng, Fast Company

item.title: A top Chinese official tours Thai-Myanmar border to highlight crackdown on scam centers
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Efforts to shut down online scam centers in Myanmar appeared to gain momentum on Monday as a top Chinese security official visited both sides of the Thai-Myanmar border ahead of expected large-scale repatriations of workers in the illicit industry.
item.dc:creator: JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Neudesic launches in Australia in a bid to speed up AI adoption for IT teams
item.description: Artificial intelligence (AI) has gained significant traction among business leaders keen to explore ways it can drive operational efficiencies and cost savings. But while top leadership is sold on its potential, it’s a different tale for IT teams working the ground.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Fetterman blasts Musk over DOGE, IRS
item.description: Sen. John Fetterman (D-Pa.) went after tech billionaire Elon Musk over a report that his Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) has been trying to access an IRS system. “I want to save billions of your money and make our government more efficient.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: How to use AI to find new movies, music, and books
item.description: This article is republished with permission from Wonder Tools, a newsletter that helps you discover the most useful sites and apps. Subscribe here. Imagine turning your reading history into a treasure map.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Caplan, Fast Company

item.title: How to set up a home network like a pro
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson helps you ensure a smooth online experience with a reliable, secure home network setup.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: GenAI can make us dumber
item.description: Generative AI (genAI) tools based on deep learning are quickly gaining adoption, but their use is raising concerns about how they affect human thought.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Downloads of DeepSeek's AI apps paused in South Korea over privacy concerns
item.description: DeepSeek, a Chinese artificial intelligence startup, has temporarily paused downloads of its chatbot apps in South Korea while it works with local authorities to address privacy concerns, according to South Korean officials on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: What changes to the CHIPS act could mean for AI growth, consumers
item.description: Since taking office, Trump has said he would place tariffs on foreign production of computer chips and semiconductors in order to return chip manufacturing to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: TikTok returns to Apple and Google app stores in the US
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — TikTok has returned to the app stores of Apple and Google in the U.S., after President Donald Trump delayed the enforcement of a TikTok ban. TikTok, which is operated by Chinese technology firm ByteDance, was removed from Apple and Google’s app stores on Jan.
item.dc:creator: ZEN SOO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Palo Alto Networks firewall bug being exploited by threat actors: Report
item.description: Admins with firewalls from Palo Alto Networks should make sure the devices are fully patched and the management interface blocked from open internet access after the discovery this week of a zero-day login authentication bypass in the PAN-OS operating system.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Mother of Little Rock homicide victim said son was killed while trying to sell shoes on Facebook Marketplace
item.description: During the last week of January, four people were killed in Little Rock, including a 19-year-old selling shoes on Facebook Marketplace.
item.dc:creator: Gary Burton Jr., NewsNation

item.title: JetBrains’ Ktor adds CLI for simpler project creation
item.description: JetBrains’ Ktor 3.1.0, an update to the Kotlin-based framework for asynchronous server-side and client-side applications, is now available, featuring a command-line tool for simpler creation of projects.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Scout Motors' effort to directly sell its electric SUVs where they'll make them stalls
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — Legislation to let Volkswagen-backed Scout Motors sell its electric SUVs directly to people in South Carolina, where the vehicles will be built, has stalled after fierce opposition from traditional automobile dealers.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KREX 5
